content:
Week 5 | Researching for a Persuasive Essay

Research
A few key strategies can help you write a strong, persuasive argument. First, be sure to choose a debatable topic you feel passionate about. Next, do your research and gather evidence to support your position. This could include data, statistics, expert opinions, and real-life examples. The more evidence you have, the stronger your argument will be. Structure your essay clearly and logically. Introduce your topic and state your thesis up front. Then, present your evidence in a series of well-organized paragraphs. Conclude by reaffirming your thesis and leaving the reader with something to consider.

Various research methods are available to help you gather evidence for your persuasive argument essay. One research method you can use is to search online for academic articles on your topic. These articles can provide valuable information and perspectives from experts in the field.

After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Credible Sources.
